# Container — Rules Summary

*Based on a document written by Benjamin Maggi at BoardGameGeek, with comments from the game's publisher.*

---

## Set-Up

- Place the Auction Island Board in the centre of the table. Place each player's board such that the Sea side of the board faces the Island (and not the player). The rest of the table is the Open Sea.
- Sort the containers by colour, and make a supply of each. For each colour, the number of containers in the supply equals 4 times the number of players. Make a supply of warehouses and of factories.
- Place the money and loan cards on the table. Since both the $10 loan cards and the $10 cash cards are purple, it is a good idea to place them on opposite sides of the Auction Island Board to reduce the risk of grabbing a loan card by mistake when making change.
- Each player receives **$20**, **1 ship**, **1 warehouse**, and **1 unique coloured factory** — no two players share a starting factory colour. The factory immediately produces one container, which is the same colour as the factory. This container is placed in their Factory Store on their playing board at the **$2 value**. The ship starts in the Open Sea.
- Deal **1 secret Container Value Card** to each player.

---

## Ending the Game

The game ends when the **second container colour is exhausted**. The ending player finishes their turn.

**Final scoring:**

Each player first determines whether they have at least one container of every colour on the Island Warehouse.
- If so: their 10/5 colour (from the secret card) scores as **$10** per container.
- If not: it scores as **$5** per container.

Each player then discards all the containers of whatever colour they have the most of on the Island Warehouse.
- If there is a tie involving their 10/5 colour, the 10/5 must go.
- Otherwise they discard the cheapest.
- Only discard containers from the Island Warehouse (not those of the discarded colour on ships or in the Harbour Store).

## Scoring

The person with the **most money** wins. Calculate your net worth as follows:

- Add the value for your Island goods, priced according to your Container Value Card.
- Add **$2** for each container in the Harbour Store, and **$3** for each on your Ship. Factory Store containers are worthless.
- Subtract **$11** for each outstanding loan.

It is possible to win the game with your cash-in-hand alone, ignoring the Island Store altogether.

---

## Loans

A player may take out a loan at any time. A player can only repay a loan during their own turn.

Taking out a loan, repaying it, and paying interest on it **do not count as actions**.

When you take out a loan you receive a $10 loan card and a matching $10 from the bank. You may have a maximum of **2 loans** outstanding at any one time.

---

## Gameplay

### Start of Turn — Pay Interest

The first thing a player must always do in their turn is to pay any interest on loans.

Interest is **10%**, so $1 for each $10 loan card. Only after paying the interest may the player repay any outstanding loan(s) to the bank.

> **Note:** The errata from the publisher (below) outlines what happens when a player cannot pay the interest. It's an utter catastrophe — don't let this happen.

### Actions Per Turn

In each turn a player performs any **2 actions** from the following list, in any order, with two caveats:

- You may only produce new containers **once per turn**.
- The auction action **always ends your turn**, even if it is the first action taken.

---

### Action (1) — Buy a Factory or a Warehouse

- Pay the bank the next amount listed on your playing board.
- You may not own two Factories of the same colour.

### Action (2) — Produce Containers and Set/Adjust Prices

- Pay **$1** to the Union Boss — the player on your right.
- You may only choose this action **once per turn**.
- You must produce 1 container per factory, in its matching colour, up to the Factory Storage limit of **2 × number of factories** you have. If you would produce over your limit, you decide which factories to leave idle. If the container supply for a particular factory is exhausted, that factory produces nothing this round.
- Place your new containers in any Factory Store space. The number on the space chosen indicates the price at which you are willing to sell the goods to other players.

**Repricing:**
- After production, you may also move all the containers in your factory storage around, repricing them.
- If you want to reprice, you must produce, even if this means taking out a loan to pay the $1 to the Union Boss because you have no money.
- If you already have 2 loans and no money, you may not reprice.
- However, if it is impossible to produce (either because the container supply for your factory colour is exhausted or because your Factory Storage is full), you may still reprice your goods using this action. In this case you do **not** pay the $1 to the player on your right.

> You may not burn your goods, toss them into the sea, or otherwise get rid of them.

### Action (3) — Buy Containers for Your Harbour Store and Set/Adjust Prices

- You may only purchase from **another player's** Factory Store, never from your own.
- You may only purchase from **one player** per action. They may not refuse to sell to you.
- You may purchase any number or types of containers, up to your storage limit of **1 container per Warehouse**.
- You pay the player their listed price for the goods in their Factory Store. When you move them to your Harbour Store you may price them at any value you choose, including less than what you paid. The number on the Harbour Store section indicates the price at which you are willing to sell these goods to other players.

**Repricing:**
- Each time you perform this action you may also reorganize all the containers in your Harbour Store, repricing them.
- You may use this action to reprice without purchasing anything.

> You may not burn your goods, toss them into the sea, or otherwise get rid of them.

### Action (4) — Move from the Open Sea to Another Player's Harbour and Purchase Containers

- You may only purchase from **another player's** Harbour Store, never your own.
- You may purchase any number or types of containers, as long as there is room for them on your ship, which can hold a **maximum of 5**. The other player may not refuse to sell to you.
- If you begin your turn in another player's Harbour and wish to ship additional goods from that player's Harbour Store (perhaps because the player has just purchased them), you may do so without first going out to Sea. Play this as if you began your action in the Open Sea. Your action ends when the additional goods are loaded — you may not load and immediately go to Sea with one action.

### Action (5) — Move from Another Player's Harbour to the Open Sea

### Action (6) — Move from the Open Sea to Auction Island and Hold an Auction

- You may not bid on your own cargo.
- You may not land an empty ship on the Island and auction a non-existent cargo.
- Each player (except yourself) secretly bids for the entire ship's cargo.

**Auction resolution:**

1. If **all players bid $0**: the Seller (you) must buy the shipment for $0 and place it on your Island Store.
2. Otherwise, the **highest bidder** wins.
3. **Tie-breaker:** each tied player makes a second secret bid of how much extra they are willing to pay (this can be $0). If the tie persists, the Seller chooses the winner.
4. The Seller then decides to **accept** or **reject** the winner's bid:
   - **Accept:** collect that amount from the winner *and* a matching amount from the bank, and deposit all the goods in the winner's Island Store.
   - **Reject:** pay the highest bid to the bank, and deposit all the goods on your own Island Store.

> Your turn ends immediately after the auction concludes. You may not repay any loans or move your Ship from the Island Harbour to the Open Sea. If your first action is to hold an auction, you forfeit your second action.

### Action (7) — Pass

Always possible, though it is very rare to want to do so.

---

## Variants (House Rules)

### Official Beginner's Variant

*(Recommended for the 5-person game at all times by the publisher, Valley Games.)*

**Additional action — (8) Domestic Sale:**

A player may take one container from their Factory Store and return it to the Container supply. If the player does not have any containers in their Factory Store, they may instead take one container from their Harbour Store. The player then collects **$2** from the bank. This action may not be used on the player's first turn of the game.

**Progressive Auction:**
- Replace blind bidding with a progressive auction, where each person around the table (beginning with the player on the seller's left) must either pass or increase the bid. This was the way the game was originally written — it was changed before release because it was felt it took too much time, though some groups report the opposite.

**Open Money:**
- Keep your money visible at all times. Some people use poker chips as cash. Reported to make a great game even better and play faster.

**Shorter Game:**
- Play with fewer containers. Opinion is divided as to whether the number of containers needs to be a multiple of the number of players.

---

## Errata — Paying Interest (Publisher: Valley Games)

*This is an official change to section 4.1 — "Pay Interest." The rulebook, as printed, has this information in a somewhat clouded manner. The following is how the section should read. — Kevin Nesbitt, one of the game's designers, on BGG.*

If a player cannot pay their interest, the bank seizes containers for each loan in default. Repeat this procedure for each defaulted loan:

1. Seize **1 container** from the delinquent player's Island Store.
2. If the player has no containers on the Island Store, seize **2 containers** from their Harbour Store.
3. If the player does not have enough containers in their Harbour Store, seize **1 container** from the Factory Store for each container not successfully seized from the Harbour Store.
4. If the player has only 1 container in play (total across Factory Store and Harbour Store), the bank seizes only **1 container** for the defaulted loan this turn.

**Example:** A player has 1 defaulted loan, 1 container in their Harbour Store, and 4 containers in their Factory Store. The bank seizes the 1 from the Harbour Store and 1 from the Factory Store, leaving 3 in the Factory Store and none in the Harbour Store. Additional defaulted loans further reduce this total.

**If the player has no containers at all:** the bank seizes either a Warehouse or a Machine. The player may then return one loan document to the bank, as it has been paid off by the asset. The player must exhaust all other measures above before allowing the seizure of a Warehouse or Machine, and may not willingly lose one unless it is the last option.

The **$0 / $6 Machine** and the **$0 / $4 Warehouse** are always safe and cannot be seized.

If the player has nothing that can be legally seized, they may avoid paying the remaining interest for the current turn only.

**Who selects what gets seized:** The player to the delinquent player's *right* makes the selection (not the delinquent player themselves). Exception: if the player is to lose a Warehouse or Machine, they may choose which type to lose. If a Machine, the player to the right still selects which colour.

Any containers seized by the bank are placed back in the box — out of the game. Any Machine or Warehouse seized is returned to the supply.
